{{Quote|It is true that in the past Major Wortin would have been executed, but we cannot afford to squander our personnel with such abandon anymore.|Ysanne Isard|<ref name="WG" />}}
'''Wortin''' was a [[Major/Legends|major]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Your report makes it quite clear that Major Wortin knew this, but took no steps to guard against a kill. And you were correct in pointing out that his insistence that the ship blew up by accident was nothing more than a sham.|Ysanne Isard to Kirtan Loor|<ref name="WG" />}}
In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Wortin served at the flight coordinator on the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor]] [[Corusca Rainbow|''Black Asp'']]. When [[Ysanne Isard]] issued orders to capture [[Bror Jace]], Wortin failed to prevent the destruction of his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. Because the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was unaware of Jace's plan to fake his own demise, it appeared that Wortin's crew had killed him. Director Isard was displeased, as she wished to imprison Jace in the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' facility for brainwashing.<ref name="WG" />

Wortin insisted that the ship had exploded by accident—which, though unknown to Imperial investigators, was actually true. [[Kirtan Loor]] prepared a detailed report on Wortin's "incompetence", and it was his opinion that Wortin should have been executed for his failure. Isard agreed with this conclusion, but the Empire needed to be more conservative in its personnel management. She decided to transfer Wortin to the [[Inexorable (Imperial I-class)|''Inexorable'']] under [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] command.<ref name="WG" />
